task_yaml.task:
I am conducting a routine internal email compliance check. The email package has already been placed in `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/mailbox/`. Please help me review these emails and see whether there are any compliance or internal-control leads that need further verification, such as conflicts of interest, approval bypasses, unclear expense categorization, vendor/payment anomalies, incomplete information retention, and similar issues.

Stay neutral and do not draw conclusions based only on keywords. First read, search, and cross-check as broadly as possible, then sort the leads you think are worth following up by suspicion level. If some leads look low-risk, also explain why.

If you find leads worth tracking, provide an evidence chain: which email ids are involved, the key evidence in the emails, why these emails are related to each other, and what evidence is still missing. Also separately mark clearly low-risk or insufficient-evidence leads, to avoid overstating normal business emails.

Save the report to `/tmp_workspace/results/email_audit_report.md`, and also output `/tmp_workspace/results/email_audit_findings.json`, listing the email id list, severity level, and one-sentence reason for each finding. Do not rely on external search as a substitute for reading the emails.
